title: arthropteris repens
intro: Arthropteris repens, also known as the creeping fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, Arthropteris repens can be made into a tea, infusion, or poultice, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
